# pluggy - A minimalist production ready plugin system

{{ pypi }} {{ conda-forge }} {{ versions }} {{ github-actions }} {{ gitter }} {{ black }} {{ codecov }}

This is the core framework used by the [pytest], [tox], and [devpi] projects.

Please [read the docs] to learn more!

## A definitive example

```python
import pluggy

hookspec = pluggy.HookspecMarker("myproject")
hookimpl = pluggy.HookimplMarker("myproject")


class MySpec:
    """A hook specification namespace."""

    @hookspec
    def myhook(self, arg1, arg2):
        """My special little hook that you can customize."""


class Plugin_1:
    """A hook implementation namespace."""

    @hookimpl
    def myhook(self, arg1, arg2):
        print("inside Plugin_1.myhook()")
        return arg1 + arg2


class Plugin_2:
    """A 2nd hook implementation namespace."""

    @hookimpl
    def myhook(self, arg1, arg2):
        print("inside Plugin_2.myhook()")
        return arg1 - arg2


# create a manager and add the spec
pm = pluggy.PluginManager("myproject")
pm.add_hookspecs(MySpec)

# register plugins
pm.register(Plugin_1())
pm.register(Plugin_2())

# call our ``myhook`` hook
results = pm.hook.myhook(arg1=1, arg2=2)
print(results)
```

Running this directly gets us:

```
$ python docs/examples/toy-example.py
inside Plugin_2.myhook()
inside Plugin_1.myhook()
[-1, 3]
```

modules.name: pluggy._version
documentation: coding: utf-8
file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

modules.name: pluggy._callers
documentation: Call loop machinery

functions.name: pluggy._callers._multicall
documentation:
Execute a call into multiple python functions/methods and return the
result(s).

`caller_kwargs` comes from \_HookCaller.\_\_call\_\_().

modules.name: pluggy._result
documentation: Hook wrapper "result" utilities.

classes.name: pluggy._result.HookCallError
documentation: Hook was called wrongly.

methods.name: pluggy._result._Result.force_result
documentation:
Force the result(s) to `result`.

If the hook was marked as a `firstresult` a single value should
be set otherwise set a (modified) list of results. Any exceptions
found during invocation will be deleted.

methods.name: pluggy._result._Result.get_result
documentation:
Get the result(s) for this hook call.

If the hook was marked as a `firstresult` only a single value
will be returned otherwise a list of results.

modules.name: pluggy._tracing
documentation: Tracing utils

modules.name: pluggy._hooks
documentation: Internal hook annotation, representation and calling machinery.

functions.name: pluggy._hooks.varnames
documentation:
Return tuple of positional and keywrord argument names for a function,
method, class or callable.

In case of a class, its `__init__` method is considered.
For methods the `self` parameter is not included.

classes.name: pluggy._hooks.HookspecMarker

methods.name: pluggy._hooks.HookspecMarker.__call__
documentation:
if passed a function, directly sets attributes on the function
which will make it discoverable to {py:meth}`.PluginManager.add_hookspecs`.
If passed no function, returns a decorator which can be applied to a function
later using the attributes supplied.

If `firstresult` is `True` the 1:N hook call (N being the number of registered
hook implementation functions) will stop at I\<=N when the I'th function
returns a non-`None` result.

If `historic` is `True` calls to a hook will be memorized and replayed
on later registered plugins.

Decorator helper class for marking functions as hook specifications.

You can instantiate it with a project_name to get a decorator.
Calling {py:meth}`.PluginManager.add_hookspecs` later will discover all marked functions
if the {py:class}`.PluginManager` uses the same project_name.

classes.name: pluggy._hooks.HookimplMarker

methods.name: pluggy._hooks.HookimplMarker.__call__
documentation:
if passed a function, directly sets attributes on the function
which will make it discoverable to {py:meth}`.PluginManager.register`.
If passed no function, returns a decorator which can be applied to a
function later using the attributes supplied.

If `optionalhook` is `True` a missing matching hook specification will not result
in an error (by default it is an error if no matching spec is found).

If `tryfirst` is `True` this hook implementation will run as early as possible
in the chain of N hook implementations for a specification.

If `trylast` is `True` this hook implementation will run as late as possible
in the chain of N hook implementations.

If `hookwrapper` is `True` the hook implementations needs to execute exactly
one `yield`.  The code before the `yield` is run early before any non-hookwrapper
function is run.  The code after the `yield` is run after all non-hookwrapper
function have run.  The `yield` receives a {py:class}`.callers._Result` object
representing the exception or result outcome of the inner calls (including other
hookwrapper calls).

If `specname` is provided, it will be used instead of the function name when
matching this hook implementation to a hook specification during registration.

Decorator helper class for marking functions as hook implementations.

You can instantiate with a `project_name` to get a decorator.
Calling {py:meth}`.PluginManager.register` later will discover all marked functions
if the {py:class}`.PluginManager` uses the same project_name.

classes.name: pluggy._hooks._HookRelay
documentation:
hook holder object for performing 1:N hook calls where N is the number
of registered plugins.

methods.name: pluggy._hooks._HookCaller._add_hookimpl
documentation: Add an implementation to the callback chain.

methods.name: pluggy._hooks._HookCaller.call_historic
documentation:
Call the hook with given `kwargs` for all registered plugins and
for all plugins which will be registered afterwards.

If `result_callback` is not `None` it will be called for for each
non-`None` result obtained from a hook implementation.

methods.name: pluggy._hooks._HookCaller.call_extra
documentation:
Call the hook with some additional temporarily participating
methods using the specified `kwargs` as call parameters.

methods.name: pluggy._hooks._HookCaller._maybe_apply_history
documentation: Apply call history to a new hookimpl if it is marked as historic.

classes.name: pluggy._manager.PluginValidationError
documentation:
plugin failed validation.

:param object plugin: the plugin which failed validation,
    may be a module or an arbitrary object.

classes.name: pluggy._manager.DistFacade
documentation: Emulate a pkg_resources Distribution

classes.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.register
documentation:
Register a plugin and return its canonical name or `None` if the name
is blocked from registering.  Raise a {py:class}`ValueError` if the plugin
is already registered.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.unregister
documentation:
unregister a plugin object and all its contained hook implementations
from internal data structures.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.set_blocked
documentation: block registrations of the given name, unregister if already registered.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.is_blocked
documentation: return `True` if the given plugin name is blocked.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.add_hookspecs
documentation:
add new hook specifications defined in the given `module_or_class`.
Functions are recognized if they have been decorated accordingly.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.get_plugins
documentation: return the set of registered plugins.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.is_registered
documentation: Return `True` if the plugin is already registered.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.get_canonical_name
documentation:
Return canonical name for a plugin object. Note that a plugin
may be registered under a different name which was specified
by the caller of {py:meth}`register(plugin, name) <.PluginManager.register>`.
To obtain the name of an registered plugin use {py:meth}`get_name(plugin)
<.PluginManager.get_name>` instead.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.get_plugin
documentation: Return a plugin or `None` for the given name.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.has_plugin
documentation: Return `True` if a plugin with the given name is registered.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.get_name
documentation: Return name for registered plugin or `None` if not registered.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.check_pending
documentation:
Verify that all hooks which have not been verified against
a hook specification are optional, otherwise raise {py:class}`.PluginValidationError`.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.load_setuptools_entrypoints
documentation:
Load modules from querying the specified setuptools `group`.

```{eval-rst}

:param str group: entry point group to load plugins
:param str name: if given, loads only plugins with the given ``name``.
:rtype: int
:return: return the number of loaded plugins by this call.
```

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.list_plugin_distinfo
documentation:
return list of distinfo/plugin tuples for all setuptools registered
plugins.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.list_name_plugin
documentation: return list of name/plugin pairs.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.get_hookcallers
documentation: get all hook callers for the specified plugin.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.add_hookcall_monitoring
documentation:
add before/after tracing functions for all hooks
and return an undo function which, when called,
will remove the added tracers.

`before(hook_name, hook_impls, kwargs)` will be called ahead
of all hook calls and receive a hookcaller instance, a list
of HookImpl instances and the keyword arguments for the hook call.

`after(outcome, hook_name, hook_impls, kwargs)` receives the
same arguments as `before` but also a {py:class}`pluggy._callers._Result` object
which represents the result of the overall hook call.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.enable_tracing
documentation: enable tracing of hook calls and return an undo function.

methods.name: pluggy._manager.PluginManager.subset_hook_caller
documentation:
Return a new {py:class}`._hooks._HookCaller` instance for the named method
which manages calls to all registered plugins except the
ones from remove_plugins.

Core {py:class}`.PluginManager` class which manages registration
of plugin objects and 1:N hook calling.

You can register new hooks by calling {py:meth}`add_hookspecs(module_or_class)
<.PluginManager.add_hookspecs>`.
You can register plugin objects (which contain hooks) by calling
{py:meth}`register(plugin) <.PluginManager.register>`.  The {py:class}`.PluginManager`
is initialized with a prefix that is searched for in the names of the dict
of registered plugin objects.

For debugging purposes you can call {py:meth}`.PluginManager.enable_tracing`
which will subsequently send debug information to the trace helper.
